properties.remark: OBSERVED WATERSPOUT OFF OF EWA BEACH
OBSERVED TWO WATERSPOUTS, ONE HAD DISSIPATED JUST AS HE CALLED.
OAHU EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT REPORTED THAT KAMEHAMEHA HIGHWAY WAS CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING NEAR THE WAIKANE STREAM CROSSING. 3 FEET OF WATER WAS REPORTED BY WAIKANE STORE.
AT ABOUT 115PM TODAY FIREFIGHTERS RESPONDED TO A MAN TRAPPED IN HIS VEHICLE BY RISING WATERS NEAR KAMEHAMEHA HIGHWAY AND WAILEHUA ROAD.
FIRE DEPARTMENT REPORTED THAT A MAN NEEDED TO BE RESCUED FROM RISING WATERS/FLASH FLOODING AT 140 RIVER ROAD.
FLASH FLOODING REPORTED ON KAHEKILI HIGHWAY ON WAIHEE BRIDGE. MUD AND WATER WAS ON THE ROAD.

KAMEHAMEHA HIGHWAY IS CLOSED IN BOTH DIRECTIONS DUE TO FLASH FLOODING. POLICE SAY THE HIGHWAY WAS CLOSED BETWEEN WAIKANE VALLEY ROAD AND KUALOA RANCH. HONOLULU FIRE CAPTAIN RECEIVED A CALL ABOUT TRAPPED MOTORISTS NEAR WAIKANE STORE AT ABOUT 110PM.
*** 1 FATAL *** DELAYED REPORT. VICTIM...A 74 YEAR OLD MAN...LOST HIS LIFE IN A SHACK-LIKE STRUCTURE WHICH GOT BLOWN 150 FEET BY THE WIND. TIME OF EVENT ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

HONOLULU FIRE DEPARTMENT REPSONDED TO A CALL OF FLOODING IN AT LEAST TWO HOMES ON THE SOUTH SIDE OF FORT WEAVER ROAD BETWEEN PARISH DRIVE AND HAPALUA STREET. A DRAINAGE CANAL HAD BECOME CLOGGED WITH DEBRIS...CAUSING WATER TO FLOOD OUT OF THE CANAL AND INTO THE HOMES. WHEN HFD ARRIVED...THE CLOG HAD CLEARED...AND WATER WAS CONFINED TO THE CANAL.
